scope: The 19 legacy curated Skills currently projected through CC Switch local rows.

authorityAndPortabilityFindings: shipping-and-launch contains a git revert plus git push rollback example and requires a separate repository-write gate
prototype directs capture through a throwaway branch and issue pointer and requires explicit Git and tracker authority
to-tickets to-spec and triage can write external tracker state and require task-bound account and mutation authority
setup-matt-pocock-skills writes repository instruction and context files after confirmation
handoff and improve-codebase-architecture write temporary artifacts and the latter opens a host GUI
code-review and improve-codebase-architecture assume named sub-agent capabilities that are not universal
git-guardrails-claude-code is explicitly Claude-specific despite carrying OpenAI display metadata
deprecation-and-migration adds categorical down-migration guidance that requires domain review for irreversible data operations
OpenAI metadata does not by itself prove cross-Agent portability or safe implicit activation
currentRevisionStaticReview.secretHandlingObservation: No embedded credential was observed in the selected exact-revision bodies; examples use placeholders or secret-store references.
currentRevisionStaticReview.decision: Keep all replacement dispositions provisional and inactive until per-Skill risk routing plus disposable activation and side-effect probes pass. Keep the three retirement dispositions unchanged.

skills.reason: Current upstream path remains active and its body is unchanged since the prior reviewed revision.

The live pool already has the distinct upstream-named git-guardrails-claude-code item; do not keep a rewritten generic duplicate unless a separately proven cross-Agent residual gap justifies a derivative.
Current upstream body is unchanged and now carries OpenAI metadata; local-only templates must not be silently merged into the source payload.
Current active upstream body should be reviewed as a whole instead of preserving the rewritten legacy body by default.
Prefer the active whole upstream tree; local-only guidance files require separate residual-gap evidence rather than silent adaptation.
Current upstream body remains stable and has a direct source-preserving path.
The source evolved as a coordinated multi-file Skill; review and consume that exact tree rather than retaining a stale rewritten fork by default.
The upstream Skill graduated from in-progress review to code-review; migration should follow the source identity and name.
The live pool already contains setup-matt-pocock-skills; the generic rewritten duplicate should not survive without a separately proven neutral setup gap.
The upstream multi-file contract materially evolved; use a current-revision delta review before exact source-backed replacement.
Follow the current upstream to-tickets identity rather than preserving the retired to-issues fork name.
Follow the current upstream to-spec identity and review its changed contract before replacement.
Current upstream body files remain stable and provide a direct whole-tree source-preserving path.
The upstream itself remains under deprecated; current native, intake, and document-grounded language capabilities must be evaluated before any exceptional retention.

requiredBeforeAnyLiveChange: current-revision static security and executable-surface review
shortfall coverage and native or installed alternative comparison
exact source-backed CC Switch preview with collision and backup plan
separate user authorization for live source registration relink replacement or retirement
post-change source identity activation backup restore and foreign-sentinel verification
